idea.hook: Mars has two tiny moons. But one is DOOMED to die! 🤯
facts: Phobos orbits Mars incredibly close, just 6,000 km (3,700 miles) above its surface.
It speeds around Mars in only 7 hours and 39 minutes – faster than Mars itself rotates!
This tight, fast orbit means Phobos is slowly spiraling inwards by about 1.8 meters (5.9 feet) per century.
idea.payoff: In just 30-50 million years, Mars will either devour Phobos whole or tear it apart into a new ring system!
idea.title: Mars's Doomed Moon! 😱🪐

scenes.text:
Mars has two tiny moons.
But one is DOOMED! 🤯
scenes.visual: A wide shot of Mars with its two small moons, Phobos and Deimos, orbiting. Focus slightly on Phobos.

scenes.text: Phobos orbits just 6,000 km (3,700 miles) above Mars' surface.
scenes.visual: Close-up view of Mars with Phobos orbiting very close. An arc visualizes its tight orbit, with a dashed line indicating the short distance.

scenes.text: It circles Mars in only 7 hours 39 minutes – faster than Mars rotates!
scenes.visual: Phobos rapidly orbiting Mars. Arrows show Phobos's fast orbital direction and Mars's slower rotational direction.

scenes.text: This tight orbit makes Phobos spiral inwards ~1.8 meters (5.9 feet) per century.
scenes.visual: Phobos on a slowly contracting spiral path towards Mars. A faint trail visually represents the inward spiral over time.

scenes.text: In 30-50 million years, Mars will DEVOUR Phobos or tear it into a new RING!
scenes.visual: A dramatic shot of Phobos nearing Mars, either breaking apart into a vibrant ring system around Mars, or being slowly engulfed by the planet's surface.
